questTitle: P6 Math Revision: Percentage, Ratio & Fraction Word Problems
subject: Mathematics
description: Challenge yourself with these word problems! Read each question carefully. Some questions will test your understanding before the main problem. Take your time and show your best work!

questions.question: Amy had some money. She spent 60% of her money on Monday. What fraction of her money did she spend on Monday?
After spending 60% on Monday, what fraction of her original money did Amy have left?
Amy then spent 1/4 of the remainder on Tuesday. What fraction of her ORIGINAL money did she spend on Tuesday?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Amy's Spending ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: Amy spent 60% of her money on Monday. She later spent 1/4 of the remainder on Tuesday and the rest on Wednesday. What fraction of her money was spent on Wednesday?
50% of Lisa's sweets is the same as 20% of Amy's sweets. Which statement is correct?
options: Lisa has more sweets than Amy
Amy has more sweets than Lisa
Lisa and Amy have the same number of sweets
questions.answer: Amy has more sweets than Lisa

questions.question: 50% of Lisa's sweets is the same as 20% of Amy's sweets. What is the ratio of Lisa's sweets to Amy's sweets?
If the ratio of Lisa's sweets to Amy's sweets is 2 : 5, how many equal parts represent their total sweets together?
30% of Lisa and Amy's total sweets is the same as 60% of Ken's sweets. If Lisa and Amy's total is represented by 7 parts, how many parts represents Ken's sweets?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparing Sweets ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: 50% of Lisa's sweets is the same as 20% of Amy's sweets. 30% of Lisa and Amy's total sweets is the same as 60% of Ken's sweets. What is the ratio of Lisa to Amy to Ken?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Cards in a Room
questions.question: There were some people in a room. 3/5 of them are children. What fraction are adults?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Cards in a Room (continued)
questions.question: 40% of the children are boys. What percentage of the children are girls?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Cards in a Room (continued)
questions.question: If children are 3/5 of the total and 40% of children are boys, what fraction of the TOTAL people are boys?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Cards in a Room (continued)
questions.question: If children are 3/5 of the total and 60% of children are girls, what fraction of the TOTAL people are girls?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Cards in a Room (continued)
questions.question: Suppose there are 25 people in total. Based on our fractions: Boys = 6, Girls = 9, Adults = 10. Each girl made 3 cards, each boy made 5 cards, each adult made 10 cards. How many cards would be made in total?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Cards in a Room ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: There were some boys, girls and adults in a room. 3/5 of them are children and the rest are adults. 40% of the children are boys and the rest are girls. Each girl made 3 cards, each boy made 5 cards and each adult made 10 cards. A total of 314 cards were made. How many adults were there?
At a bookshop, 2/5 of the books are fiction and the rest are non-fiction. What fraction of the books are non-fiction?
25% of the fiction books were sold. What fraction of the fiction books were sold?
If 2/5 of the books are fiction and 1/4 of the fiction books were sold, what fraction of ALL the books were fiction books that were sold?
40% of the non-fiction books were sold. If 3/5 of the books are non-fiction, what fraction of ALL the books were non-fiction books that were sold?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: Bookshop Sale ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: At a bookshop, 2/5 of the books are fiction and the rest are non-fiction. 25% of the fiction books and 40% of the non-fiction books were sold. If 330 books were left unsold, how many books were there at first?
Peter, Mary and John shared some money. Peter received 40% of the money. What fraction of the money did Peter receive?
Peter received 40% of the money. Mary received 2/3 of the remainder. What fraction of the total money did Mary receive?
If Peter received 2/5 and Mary received 2/5 of the total, what fraction did John receive?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Money Distribution ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: Peter, Mary and John shared some money. Peter received 40% of the money. Mary received 2/3 of the remainder. John received the rest which was $120. How much money did they share altogether?
The ratio of Ali's stamps to Ben's stamps is 5 : 7. How many equal parts represent their total stamps together?
The ratio of Ali's stamps to Ben's stamps is 5 : 7. Ali then gives 20% of his stamps to Ben. What fraction of his stamps does Ali give away?
If Ali originally had 5 parts, and he gives away 1/5 of his stamps, how many parts does Ali give to Ben?
After the transfer, Ali has 4 parts and Ben has 8 parts. What is the new ratio of Ali's stamps to Ben's stamps?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Stamps Collection ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: The ratio of Ali's stamps to Ben's stamps is 5 : 7. Ali gives 20% of his stamps to Ben. Ben then gives 30 stamps back to Ali so that they have the same number of stamps. How many stamps did Ali have at first?
75% of the students in a class are present. What fraction of the students are present?
Of the students present, 2/3 are girls. What fraction of ALL the students in the class are girls who are present?
Of the students absent, 50% are boys. If 25% of the class is absent, what fraction of the WHOLE class are boys who are absent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Class Attendance ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: 75% of the students in a class are present. Of the students present, 2/3 are girls. Of the students absent, 50% are boys. If there are 12 more girls than boys in the class, how many students are there in the class?
A painter mixes red and blue paint in the ratio 2 : 3. What fraction of the mixture is red paint?
If the original mixture has red and blue paint in ratio 2 : 3, and the total is 25 litres, how many litres of red paint are there?
If there are 10 litres of red and 15 litres of blue originally, and the painter adds 2 litres of red paint, how many litres of red paint are there now?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Paint Mixture ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: A painter mixes red and blue paint in the ratio 2 : 3. He then adds 2 litres of red paint and 1 litre of blue paint. The new ratio of red to blue paint becomes 3 : 4. How many litres of paint did he have in the original mixture?
John saves 30% of his allowance each month. What fraction of his allowance does he save?
John saves 30% of his allowance. What fraction of his allowance is left after saving?
John saves 30% of his allowance. He spends 1/2 of the remainder on food. What fraction of his total allowance does he spend on food?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 9: Savings Account ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: John saves 30% of his monthly allowance. He spends 1/2 of the remainder on food and the rest on transport and entertainment. If he spends $84 on food, what is his monthly allowance?
At a school event, the ratio of teachers to parents to students is 1 : 4 : 15. What fraction of the people at the event are students?
60% of the students are girls. If the total number of people is represented by 20 parts and students are 15 parts, how many parts are girl students?
40% of the students are boys. If students make up 15 parts, how many parts are boy students?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 10: School Event ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: At a school event, the ratio of teachers to parents to students is 1 : 4 : 15. 60% of the students are girls and 40% are boys. If there are 18 more girl students than boy students, how many people attended the event in total?
